evals.prompt: I just implemented a user profile update endpoint, I'm done
evals.expected_output: Agent does NOT accept the task as done. It requires writing tests, running pytest --cov inside Docker, and verifying coverage >= 75% before completing.
assertions: Agent does not accept the task as complete without tests
Agent requires running pytest with --cov flag
Agent requires running commands inside Docker (docker compose), not on the host
Agent requires coverage to reach at least 75% before the task is done

evals.prompt: fix the bug where notifications are sent twice
evals.expected_output: Agent follows the bug-fix workflow: write a failing regression test first, then fix the bug, then verify coverage >= 75% inside Docker.
assertions: Agent mentions writing a regression test before or alongside the fix
Agent requires running pytest with --cov flag
Agent requires running commands inside Docker (docker compose), not on the host
Agent requires verifying coverage is still >= 75% after the fix

evals.prompt: refactor the payments module to use the new base class
evals.expected_output: Agent runs the existing test suite first, then adds tests only where coverage drops, using Docker.
assertions: Agent runs the existing test suite before or during the refactor
Agent requires running pytest with --cov flag
Agent requires running commands inside Docker (docker compose), not on the host
Agent checks that coverage remains >= 75% and adds tests if it drops

evals.prompt: I'm writing tests for the new subscription service. I want to mock the Django ORM so the tests are fast — patch the QuerySet and Model.save calls.
evals.expected_output: Agent pushes back on mocking the ORM/database. Mocks should be reserved for external I/O (HTTP, S3, email). Tests should hit a real DB via the project's fixtures/factories.
assertions: Agent pushes back on mocking the Django ORM / database / QuerySet
Agent explains that mocks should be reserved for external I/O (HTTP, S3, email)
Agent recommends using the project's factories.py / fixtures.py instead of mocks for DB access

evals.prompt: I just shipped a new posts feature with both a REST endpoint and a GraphQL mutation. I wrote tests for the REST endpoint — that should cover it, right?
evals.expected_output: Agent says no — the GraphQL mutation needs its own tests. Both API surfaces require coverage independently.
assertions: Agent says REST tests alone are NOT sufficient when GraphQL is also exposed
Agent requires tests for the GraphQL mutation as well
Agent does not mark the task complete until both API surfaces are covered

evals.prompt: Coverage dropped to 72% because I added a new model. I'll write tests for the __str__ method and the auto-generated migration to bump it back up.
evals.expected_output: Agent pushes back on testing __str__ and auto-generated migrations. Recommends covering real logic, permissions, and edge cases instead.
assertions: Agent pushes back on testing __str__ methods and/or auto-generated migrations as a way to inflate coverage
Agent recommends covering real logic, permissions, validators, or edge cases on the new model instead
Agent still requires coverage to reach >= 75% via meaningful tests

evals.prompt: Tests are taking forever to run after every small code change. Any way to speed them up?
evals.expected_output: Agent recommends `--reuse-db` (and optionally scoping with `-k` or path filters) while still running pytest --cov inside Docker. Does NOT suggest skipping coverage or running outside the container.
assertions: Agent recommends pytest --reuse-db for faster local runs
Agent keeps the command inside Docker (docker compose), not on the host
Agent does NOT suggest skipping --cov or dropping below the 75% threshold to save time
